$comment: Phase 4.28i — single source of truth for compensation/margin vocabulary that must not appear in any new source change. The pre-commit hook (scripts/check-sensitive-vocab.js) and the CI workflow (.github/workflows/sensitive-vocab.yml) both consume this file. Sync this list with the runtime guard in backend/services/brandSafetyGateway.js if you add or remove patterns.
$comment_allowlist: Files where the words are STRUCTURAL — the model, service, controller, migration, seed, AI prompt, or skill file that legitimately implements / documents the compensation system. Adding paths here is allowed; weakening the patterns is not.
$comment_allowlist_dirs: Whole-directory allowlists. Files inside these dirs are exempt from scanning. The scope here is INTERNAL / OPERATOR-FACING surfaces — admin portal, mobile ops app, docs, skills, models, migrations, services. The scanner stays strict on BUYER-FACING and FACTORY-FACING surfaces: frontend/customer-portal/ + frontend/factory-portal/ + the website repo (separate). The runtime brandSafetyGateway covers the data layer for those surfaces. The git-time scanner covers the source layer.
$comment_strict_dirs: Surfaces the scanner stays strict on. Everything under these dirs is scanned. These are the directories whose source code renders strings directly to buyers or factories. Listed here for documentation; the scanner currently treats them as 'not allowlisted' rather than reading this field. Keep this list in sync with reality — add new buyer/factory-facing app directories as they appear.
